feed.title: The Ouroboros Protocol - Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (draft)

entry.title: The Ouroboros Protocol - Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (draft)
entry.summary:
The proposed "**The Ouroboros Protocol**" aims to address multiple persistent issues within the Bitcoin protocol by introducing changes that ensure perpetual security. This Draft Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (BIP) targets problems such as abandoned Unspent Transaction Outputs (UTXOs), challenges related to long-term security of the blockchain, the management of small transaction outputs or "dust", and potential technological obsolescence. Additionally, it touches upon concerns regarding the 2106 hard fork event.
The motivation behind exploring this protocol stems from the inherent risks associated with the current transition to a fee-market model, which is predicted to affect blockchain's stability due to erratic hashrate behaviors and fee sniping vulnerabilities. The standard voluntary mechanisms like "State Rent" are criticized for their inability to manage permanently lost coins and dust effectively, which results in increased storage burdens on full nodes without any resolution to clearing dead data.
"The Ouroboros Protocol" proposes an active UTXO renewal process to mitigate these issues, including reducing risks related to quantum attacks on early-era P2PK outputs, often referred to as the "quantum honey-pot." For those interested in further details or wish to provide feedback on potential flaws or improvements, the full draft of the BIP can be accessed and reviewed on the GitHub repository [here](https://github.com/bip-proposer/bitcoin-infinity/blob/5517fa9e5da1667c786dc46cfc11c72d4c0bfa77/ouroboros.md).
